Browse by flow

Switch interview mode

4 flows
High-level design interview prep HLD
Architecture, distributed systems trade-offs, and end-to-end system walkthroughs for classic system design rounds.
Low-level design interview prep LLD
Object modeling, extensible APIs, machine-coding structure, and follow-up handling for class-design rounds.
Data structures and algorithms interview prep DSA
Curated coding-round practice across general problem sets and company-specific question banks for array, tree, graph, DP, and mock-round prep.
AI Engineer learning path AI/ML
End-to-end AI/ML engineering from foundations through LLMs, RAG, agents, MLOps, and responsible AI for building production intelligent systems.

Curriculum → AI Engineer learning path → AI agents and autonomous systems

AI/ML learning path

AI agents and autonomous systems

Design, build, and deploy AI agents that reason, plan, use tools, and accomplish multi-step tasks autonomously.

Module progress 0%

0 of 3 lessons complete

Next up Lesson 1: Agent architectures and patterns

ReAct, plan-and-execute, reflection, and multi-agent patterns for building autonomous AI systems.

Why this module matters Build one clean mental model before scaling it.

Move through the lessons in order so each trade-off has context before the harder distributed systems decisions show up.

Study target 3 lessons left

Aim to finish one lesson, then explain its core trade-off out loud before moving on.

AI Engineer learning path 3 lessons 0/3 complete Follow in order for best results Back to AI/ML roadmap Start module View lesson runway

Learning rhythm

Study this like a sequenced course, not a reference dump

  1. Preview the runway. Skim every lesson title first so you can see the progression before you dive in.
  2. Do one lesson deeply. Read the summary, inspect the diagram, and identify the sharpest trade-off.
  3. Say it back aloud. Explain the idea in interviewer language before you mark it complete.
  4. Only then advance. Move to the next lesson when you can defend the previous one without the notes open.
AI/ML sequence Designed for interview recall

Outcomes

What you should walk away with

  • Understand agent architectures: ReAct, plan-and-execute, and multi-agent systems
  • Implement tool use, memory, and planning capabilities in LLM-powered agents
  • Evaluate agent reliability, safety, and cost in production deployments

Practice flow

How to study each lesson

  • Read the summary and explain the core trade-off in your own words.
  • Study the diagram and identify the critical path or failure boundary.
  • Answer the interview prompts aloud before marking the lesson complete.

Mastery check

How you know it is sticking

  • Clear assumptions and estimated scale.
  • Justified component choices instead of pattern dumping.
  • Awareness of operations, failure modes, and trade-offs.

Lesson runway

Follow the sequence from first principles to fluency

This is the core pattern borrowed from stronger learning products: show the path, make the next step obvious, and keep outcomes visible while the learner moves.

Tool use and function calling

25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections

Lesson 1

Agent architectures and patterns

Open

ReAct, plan-and-execute, reflection, and multi-agent patterns for building autonomous AI systems.

Why it matters

AI agents represent the next frontier of LLM applications, moving from single-turn generation to multi-step task completion.

What you will cover

What makes an agentArchitecture patternsMulti-agent systems

Good interview signal

  • Can explain the core agent loop and how it differs from single-turn LLM usage.
  • Understands tradeoffs between ReAct and plan-and-execute patterns.
30-40 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 2

Tool use and function calling

Open

Designing tool interfaces, function schemas, error handling, and safety guardrails for agents that interact with external systems.

Why it matters

Tools transform LLMs from text generators into capable actors that can search, compute, access databases, and control external services.

What you will cover

Designing tool interfacesError handling and recoverySafety and permissions

Good interview signal

  • Can design tool schemas that minimize LLM invocation errors.
  • Implements proper error handling and fallback strategies for tool failures.
25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 3

Agent evaluation and safety

Open

Benchmarking agent performance, measuring reliability, and implementing safety controls for production deployment.

Why it matters

Agents operating autonomously can cause real harm if not properly evaluated and constrained. Safety is not optional for production systems.

What you will cover

Evaluation frameworksSafety mechanismsProduction deployment

Good interview signal

  • Has an evaluation suite that measures task success, cost, and safety metrics.
  • Implements budget limits, content filtering, and audit logging.
25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson
